Acrobatics.description: The character dives, rolls, or somersaults through a narrow opening, between legs, or past an obstacle.
Acrobatics.success: The character passes through cleanly, landing on their feet on the other side without losing momentum.
Acrobatics.failure: The character misjudges and stumbles, ending up prone or blocked, wasting their action.
Acrobatics.phenomenal_success: The character flows through with such grace they can immediately act again this turn without penalty.
Acrobatics.catastrophic_failure: The character crashes headlong into the obstacle, taking 1d6 crushing damage and landing in a heap.

Acrobatics.description: When falling or being knocked down, the character uses trained technique to absorb impact and recover quickly.
Acrobatics.success: Effective falling distance is reduced by 5 yards for damage purposes; the character rolls upright and can act normally.
Acrobatics.failure: The character takes full falling damage and lands prone.
Acrobatics.phenomenal_success: The character takes no damage whatsoever and lands standing, unperturbed.
Acrobatics.catastrophic_failure: The character lands at a terrible angle, taking full damage plus an additional 1d6 and possibly spraining a limb.

Acrobatics.description: The character walks across a rope, beam, or narrow surface above dangerous ground.
Acrobatics.success: The character crosses safely, reaching the other side without incident.
Acrobatics.failure: The character wobbles and must grab the rope to stop from falling, hanging helplessly.
Acrobatics.phenomenal_success: The character crosses with stunning sureness and speed, halving the crossing time.
Acrobatics.catastrophic_failure: The character plummets off the rope immediately, taking full falling damage.

Acrobatics.description: In combat, the character leaps or rolls dramatically to avoid an incoming attack.
Acrobatics.success: The attack misses entirely; the character ends up in a better position.
Acrobatics.failure: The character's dodge fails and they are hit normally.
Acrobatics.phenomenal_success: The dodge moves the character behind the attacker, granting a free attack.
Acrobatics.catastrophic_failure: The character stumbles into the blow, taking damage with no defence, and falls prone.

Acting.description: The character performs a convincing emotional display — grief, joy, fear — to deceive observers.
Acting.success: Observers believe the emotion is genuine and react accordingly.
Acting.failure: The performance rings hollow; onlookers are not convinced.
Acting.phenomenal_success: The display is so powerful it provokes genuine sympathy or the desired reaction in the targets.
Acting.catastrophic_failure: The attempt is obviously fake; observers become suspicious of everything the character says afterward.

Acting.description: The character collapses and convincingly mimics unconsciousness or death during or after a fight.
Acting.success: Enemies or witnesses believe the character is incapacitated and leave them or lower their guard.
Acting.failure: An enemy notices movement or breathing and knows the ruse.
Acting.phenomenal_success: Even a close inspection fails to reveal the deception; the character may act freely on a later turn.
Acting.catastrophic_failure: The character is so unconvincing that observers immediately attack to make sure.

Acting.description: The character adopts mannerisms, voice, and behaviour to convincingly pose as a specific individual.
Acting.success: Casual acquaintances are fooled and interact with the character as if they were the real person.
Acting.failure: Something feels off; the target of deception grows suspicious and may confront the character.
Acting.phenomenal_success: Close acquaintances of the impersonated person are fully convinced for the duration of the encounter.
Acting.catastrophic_failure: The impersonation is immediately exposed, likely causing alarm and hostility from those present.

Acting.description: Over the course of a prolonged conversation, the character maintains a false identity or story under scrutiny.
Acting.success: The character keeps all details consistent; the listener remains convinced.
Acting.failure: The character makes a telling inconsistency; the listener grows suspicious.
Acting.phenomenal_success: The character so fully embodies the lie that even probing questions are smoothly deflected.
Acting.catastrophic_failure: A major slip reveals the deception outright; the listener is now actively hostile.

Administration.description: The character deals with officials, clerks, or guild administrators to obtain permits, access, or information.
Administration.success: The paperwork or request is processed without obstruction; the character gets what they need.
Administration.failure: The official refuses or demands more documentation, causing delay.
Administration.phenomenal_success: The request is processed immediately with a favorable reaction bonus for future dealings.
Administration.catastrophic_failure: The character inadvertently draws official scrutiny or suspicion, potentially leading to investigation.

Administration.description: The character plans and coordinates the procurement and distribution of goods for an expedition, army, or estate.
Administration.success: Supplies arrive on time and in adequate quantity; costs are controlled.
Administration.failure: Shortages or delays arise, causing hardship for those depending on the supplies.
Administration.phenomenal_success: The supply operation runs so smoothly that costs are reduced by 20% and morale improves.
Administration.catastrophic_failure: A catastrophic mismanagement leads to spoilage, theft, or late delivery at critical moment.

Administration.description: The character reviews ledgers and accounts to determine if finances are in order or if fraud has occurred.
Administration.success: The character identifies whether the books are honest and spots any obvious irregularities.
Administration.failure: The character misses important discrepancies and declares the records clean.
Administration.phenomenal_success: The character finds hidden embezzlement or fraud, along with enough evidence to prove it.
Administration.catastrophic_failure: The character declares fraudulent books clean, or accuses an innocent party of wrongdoing.

Alchemy.description: The character prepares a medicinal salve or poultice from herbs and alchemical compounds to aid recovery.
Alchemy.success: The unguent is correctly formulated; when applied, it provides a bonus to healing rolls.
Alchemy.failure: The mixture is inert and provides no benefit.
Alchemy.phenomenal_success: The preparation is unusually potent, providing a significant healing bonus and reducing recovery time.
Alchemy.catastrophic_failure: The mixture is tainted; applying it causes skin irritation or mild poisoning.

Alchemy.description: The character examines and tests a mysterious compound to determine its composition and properties.
Alchemy.success: The character correctly identifies the substance and its primary uses or dangers.
Alchemy.failure: The character cannot determine the substance's nature.
Alchemy.phenomenal_success: The character fully characterises the substance, including subtle effects and precise origin.
Alchemy.catastrophic_failure: The character misidentifies the substance entirely, potentially causing harm through misuse.

Alchemy.description: The character follows an alchemical formula to create a complex compound such as a stimulant or sleep draught.
Alchemy.success: The elixir is correctly prepared and will function as intended when administered.
Alchemy.failure: The elixir is impotent; it has no effect.
Alchemy.phenomenal_success: The elixir is especially pure and potent, its effects lasting twice as long or being twice as strong.
Alchemy.catastrophic_failure: The elixir is dangerously wrong; ingesting it causes serious illness or worse.

Alchemy.description: Using alchemical and metallurgical knowledge, the character purifies ore to extract usable metal.
Alchemy.success: The character extracts a normal yield of refined metal from the ore.
Alchemy.failure: The process wastes material; yield is reduced by half.
Alchemy.phenomenal_success: The character extracts an exceptional yield, recovering 50% more metal than expected.
Alchemy.catastrophic_failure: The process ruins the ore completely, destroying all usable material and potentially starting a fire.

Algebra.description: The character works through a lord's or merchant's financial accounts to determine income, expenditure, and tax owed.
Algebra.success: The ledger is correctly calculated; totals are accurate and any errors found.
Algebra.failure: The character makes a significant arithmetic error that goes unnoticed.
Algebra.phenomenal_success: The calculation is flawless and reveals opportunities for financial savings or hidden income.
Algebra.catastrophic_failure: A serious error causes the accounts to be wildly wrong, potentially leading to underpayment of taxes or false accusations.

Algebra.description: The character applies mathematical reasoning to determine whether a bridge, floor, or wall can bear a given weight.
Algebra.success: The estimate is accurate within acceptable margins; engineering decisions can safely proceed.
Algebra.failure: The estimate is somewhat off; minor unforeseen stress occurs.
Algebra.phenomenal_success: The calculation is precise enough to allow optimisation of materials, saving cost or weight.
Algebra.catastrophic_failure: The calculation is fundamentally wrong; a structure built or relied on to this specification will fail catastrophically.

Algebra.description: The character scrutinises financial records with mathematical precision to identify tampering or embezzlement.
Algebra.success: The character finds the fraudulent entries and can demonstrate the discrepancy.
Algebra.failure: The character finds no evidence, either because records are clean or the fraud was well hidden.
Algebra.phenomenal_success: The character traces the fraud to its source and estimates the full amount stolen.
Algebra.catastrophic_failure: The character declares the fraudulent books accurate, potentially implicating an innocent party instead.

Animal Handling.description: The handler works with an animal daily to teach it a specific task, command, or behaviour.
Animal Handling.success: The animal learns and will reliably perform the task on command.
Animal Handling.failure: The animal makes no progress; today's training session is wasted.
Animal Handling.phenomenal_success: The animal learns the task unusually quickly and may begin learning a follow-up command sooner.
Animal Handling.catastrophic_failure: The animal attacks or bolts, and may become harder to work with in the future.

Animal Handling.description: The handler uses voice, posture, and knowledge of animal behaviour to soothe a distressed or panicking creature.
Animal Handling.success: The animal calms down, ceases frantic behaviour, and becomes manageable.
Animal Handling.failure: The animal remains distressed and unresponsive to the handler's efforts.
Animal Handling.phenomenal_success: The animal calms instantly and becomes unusually trusting toward the handler.
Animal Handling.catastrophic_failure: The attempt panics the animal further; it attacks, bolts, or bites.

Animal Handling.description: The handler issues a command to a trained animal, directing it to perform a known task such as tracking, fetching, or guarding.
Animal Handling.success: The animal performs the task reliably and effectively.
Animal Handling.failure: The animal hesitates, misunderstands, or ignores the command.
Animal Handling.phenomenal_success: The animal performs the task with exceptional speed and precision.
Animal Handling.catastrophic_failure: The animal disobeys completely, potentially doing the opposite of what was intended.

Animal Handling.description: The handler applies veterinary knowledge to clean wounds, set a broken limb, or administer remedies to a sick or injured animal.
Animal Handling.success: The animal's injury is properly treated; it gains a bonus to natural recovery.
Animal Handling.failure: The treatment has no effect; the animal must recover on its own.
Animal Handling.phenomenal_success: The treatment is so effective the animal recovers significantly faster and without complications.
Animal Handling.catastrophic_failure: The handler injures the animal further or introduces infection, worsening its condition.

Architecture.description: The character examines a structure to determine its soundness, identifying cracks, weak foundations, or deterioration.
Architecture.success: The character accurately identifies the structural weaknesses and can recommend repairs.
Architecture.failure: The character finds nothing or misses a critical flaw.
Architecture.phenomenal_success: The character identifies all weaknesses, estimates the time before failure, and proposes cost-effective repairs.
Architecture.catastrophic_failure: The character declares a dangerously unsound building to be safe, or vice versa.

Architecture.description: The character plans walls, towers, and gates suitable for a settlement's defence against military assault.
Architecture.success: The design is sound; when built, it will provide the expected defensive benefits.
Architecture.failure: The design has significant flaws that will require expensive revision before construction.
Architecture.phenomenal_success: The design incorporates clever features that make the fortification unusually difficult to assault.
Architecture.catastrophic_failure: The design contains a fundamental error — blind spots, inadequate foundations — that an attacker could easily exploit.

Architecture.description: The character produces a workable plan for a hall, church, mill, or other structure suited to its purpose.
Architecture.success: The plan is practical and well-suited to the patron's needs; construction can begin.
Architecture.failure: The plan has deficiencies that require revision, costing time and resources.
Architecture.phenomenal_success: The plan is elegant and efficient, reducing construction cost or time by 20%.
Architecture.catastrophic_failure: The plan contains a serious flaw; if built as drawn, the structure will be unsafe or unusable.

Architecture.description: Using architectural knowledge, the character searches a building's walls and floors for concealed doors or passages.
Architecture.success: The character locates the hidden entrance and understands how it operates.
Architecture.failure: The character finds nothing, though a passage may still exist.
Architecture.phenomenal_success: The character locates not just the passage but all hidden features of the room.
Architecture.catastrophic_failure: The character triggers a trap integrated into the concealed mechanism.

Area Knowledge.description: Using familiarity with a region, the character guides a group through unfamiliar local roads, paths, and landmarks.
Area Knowledge.success: The group travels efficiently, avoiding wrong turns and reaching their destination without undue delay.
Area Knowledge.failure: The character gives uncertain guidance, and the group loses time backtracking.
Area Knowledge.phenomenal_success: The character finds a shortcut that cuts travel time by a quarter.
Area Knowledge.catastrophic_failure: The character confidently leads the group down a wrong path, wasting hours or into a dangerous area.

Area Knowledge.description: The character thinks through who they know in the region — merchants, officials, innkeepers — who might be of use.
Area Knowledge.success: The character names a relevant and accessible contact who can provide what is needed.
Area Knowledge.failure: The character cannot think of anyone suitable in the area.
Area Knowledge.phenomenal_success: The character recalls multiple useful contacts and remembers a favour owed.
Area Knowledge.catastrophic_failure: The character misremembers, leading the party to someone hostile or no longer in the area.

Area Knowledge.description: The character uses regional knowledge to warn of known hazards — bandits, floods, disease — affecting a route.
Area Knowledge.success: The character accurately identifies the hazard and can suggest how to avoid or prepare for it.
Area Knowledge.failure: The character is uncertain or unaware of the danger.
Area Knowledge.phenomenal_success: The character knows the hazard in detail and provides information that allows the group to completely avoid it.
Area Knowledge.catastrophic_failure: The character believes a known dangerous route to be safe, leading others into peril.

Armoury (Armour).description: The armourer mends dents, broken straps, torn mail links, or other damage sustained in battle.
Armoury (Armour).success: The armour is restored to full DR and HP.
Armoury (Armour).failure: The repair is only partial; the armour's DR remains reduced by 1.
Armoury (Armour).phenomenal_success: The armour is repaired perfectly and the character spots another flaw that is also fixed at no extra cost.
Armoury (Armour).catastrophic_failure: The repair worsens the damage or introduces a new weakness that may fail at a critical moment.

Armoury (Armour).action: Fit Armour to a New Wearer
Armoury (Armour).description: The armourer adjusts straps, padding, and plates so that armour intended for one person fits another correctly.
Armoury (Armour).success: The armour fits the new wearer properly; no penalties to movement or comfort.
Armoury (Armour).failure: The fit is imperfect; the wearer suffers minor discomfort and a small skill penalty.
Armoury (Armour).phenomenal_success: The fit is excellent; the armour moves so naturally it may feel as though it was made for the new wearer.
Armoury (Armour).catastrophic_failure: The adjustment creates binding or gaps; the wearer suffers an additional -1 to DX and reduced DR in one location.

Armoury (Armour).description: The armourer examines a piece of armour to assess its quality, origin, and true protective value.
Armoury (Armour).success: The character accurately identifies the armour's DR, quality, and approximate value.
Armoury (Armour).failure: The character gives only a rough estimate and may over- or under-value the piece.
Armoury (Armour).phenomenal_success: The character identifies the armour's maker, region of origin, and any hidden flaws or exceptional craftsmanship.
Armoury (Armour).catastrophic_failure: The character badly misjudges the armour, potentially leading to a dangerous purchase or sale.

Armoury (Gunpowder).description: The character measures and prepares a correct charge of black powder for a handgonne or cannon.
Armoury (Gunpowder).success: The charge is correctly measured and safely prepared for use.
Armoury (Gunpowder).failure: The charge is slightly off; the weapon may misfire or have reduced effectiveness.
Armoury (Gunpowder).phenomenal_success: The charge is perfectly optimised for maximum safe power and reliability.
Armoury (Gunpowder).catastrophic_failure: The charge is dangerously overloaded or contaminated; the weapon may burst on firing.

Armoury (Gunpowder).description: The character cleans, inspects, and maintains a handgonne to keep it in reliable working order.
Armoury (Gunpowder).success: The weapon is clean, free of fouling, and reliable for the next engagement.
Armoury (Gunpowder).failure: The service misses fouling; the weapon has increased misfire chance.
Armoury (Gunpowder).phenomenal_success: The weapon is in exceptional condition; its misfire chance is reduced for the next day.
Armoury (Gunpowder).catastrophic_failure: The maintenance damages the touch hole or mechanism; the weapon cannot fire until properly repaired.

Armoury (Melee Weapons).description: The character uses whetstone and honing tools to restore a blade's edge after use or neglect.
Armoury (Melee Weapons).success: The weapon is sharpened to full effectiveness; cutting damage is restored.
Armoury (Melee Weapons).failure: The edge remains dull; no benefit gained.
Armoury (Melee Weapons).phenomenal_success: The blade is honed to exceptional sharpness; it gains a temporary +1 cutting damage bonus.
Armoury (Melee Weapons).catastrophic_failure: The character grinds unevenly, chipping the blade and permanently weakening it.

Armoury (Melee Weapons).description: The character re-hafts a spear, welds a broken sword, or replaces a damaged hilt on a melee weapon.
Armoury (Melee Weapons).success: The weapon is restored to full HP and function.
Armoury (Melee Weapons).failure: The repair holds but is fragile; the weapon has reduced HP.
Armoury (Melee Weapons).phenomenal_success: The repair is indistinguishable from new; full HP restored and the repair point is actually stronger than before.
Armoury (Melee Weapons).catastrophic_failure: The repair fails structurally; the weapon will break again at the worst possible moment.

Armoury (Melee Weapons).description: The character forges or fashions a basic weapon such as a knife, spear, or axe from available materials.
Armoury (Melee Weapons).success: The weapon is functional and of average quality; it performs as a normal weapon of its type.
Armoury (Melee Weapons).failure: The weapon is poorly made; it has -1 to damage or is fragile.
Armoury (Melee Weapons).phenomenal_success: The weapon is of fine quality; it gains +1 damage or a DR bonus against breakage.
Armoury (Melee Weapons).catastrophic_failure: The weapon is fundamentally flawed and will break on first serious use.

Armoury (Missile Weapons).description: The character cuts, trims, and attaches feather fletching to arrow shafts to ensure stable flight.
Armoury (Missile Weapons).success: A quiver of well-balanced arrows is produced; they fly accurately and reliably.
Armoury (Missile Weapons).failure: The fletching is uneven; the arrows suffer a small accuracy penalty.
Armoury (Missile Weapons).phenomenal_success: The arrows are masterfully balanced; they grant a small bonus to the archer's skill for this batch.
Armoury (Missile Weapons).catastrophic_failure: The arrows are so poorly balanced they veer wildly; they impose a -2 accuracy penalty.

Armoury (Missile Weapons).description: The character re-strings or adjusts a bow's string tension for optimal performance.
Armoury (Missile Weapons).success: The bow is strung correctly; full range and damage as normal.
Armoury (Missile Weapons).failure: The tension is slightly off; the bow has slightly reduced range.
Armoury (Missile Weapons).phenomenal_success: The bow is perfectly strung; it gains a small bonus to damage for this use period.
Armoury (Missile Weapons).catastrophic_failure: The string snaps under improper tension, potentially lashing the character's face.

Armoury (Missile Weapons).description: The character repairs a broken prod, nut, or spanning mechanism on a crossbow.
Armoury (Missile Weapons).success: The crossbow is restored to working order.
Armoury (Missile Weapons).failure: The repair is temporary; the weapon works but may jam under heavy use.
Armoury (Missile Weapons).phenomenal_success: The repair improves the mechanism; cocking time is reduced slightly.
Armoury (Missile Weapons).catastrophic_failure: The repair fails; the crossbow dry-fires on first use, potentially snapping the prod.

Artist (Body Art).description: The character uses needles and pigment to permanently mark a design on the subject's skin.
Artist (Body Art).success: The tattoo is clear, properly healed, and faithful to the intended design.
Artist (Body Art).failure: The lines are uneven or blurry; the result is aesthetically poor.
Artist (Body Art).phenomenal_success: The tattoo is a work of art that provokes admiration and may serve as a social asset.
Artist (Body Art).catastrophic_failure: The technique is so rough it causes unnecessary scarring or infection risk.

Artist (Body Art).description: The character paints traditional symbols or clan markings on a person's face or body for ceremony.
Artist (Body Art).success: The paint is applied correctly; the subject is appropriately prepared for the ritual or ceremony.
Artist (Body Art).failure: The symbols are imprecise; knowledgeable observers may consider it disrespectful.
Artist (Body Art).phenomenal_success: The body paint is stunning and considered a blessing; reaction bonus for the ceremony.
Artist (Body Art).catastrophic_failure: The symbols are applied wrongly; the result may be considered offensive or inauspicious.

Artist (Calligraphy).action: Copy a Document in Fine Script
Artist (Calligraphy).description: The character transcribes a letter, charter, or manuscript in careful, beautiful handwriting.
Artist (Calligraphy).success: The copy is legible, attractive, and commands respect as a formal document.
Artist (Calligraphy).failure: The script is messy or contains errors; it may need to be redone.
Artist (Calligraphy).phenomenal_success: The calligraphy is exquisite; the document impresses recipients and gains credibility.
Artist (Calligraphy).catastrophic_failure: The character makes a transcription error that changes the meaning of the text.

Artist (Calligraphy).description: The character adds decorative borders, drop caps, and ornamentation to a letter or official document.
Artist (Calligraphy).success: The illumination is attractive and appropriate; the document is impressive.
Artist (Calligraphy).failure: The decoration is amateur and detracts from the document's authority.
Artist (Calligraphy).phenomenal_success: The illuminated document is considered a minor work of art; it increases the prestige of its sender.
Artist (Calligraphy).catastrophic_failure: An ink spill or botched brush stroke ruins the document entirely.

Artist (Drawing).description: The character produces a recognisable likeness of a subject in charcoal or ink.
Artist (Drawing).success: The portrait is a good likeness; people who know the subject will recognise it.
Artist (Drawing).failure: The portrait is too generic to be useful for identification.
Artist (Drawing).phenomenal_success: The portrait captures the subject's character so well it can aid in social interactions involving the subject.
Artist (Drawing).catastrophic_failure: The portrait is so unlike the subject it may cause the wrong person to be mistaken for them.

Artist (Drawing).description: The character produces a clear plan or diagram of a mechanism, building, or region for practical use.
Artist (Drawing).success: The diagram is accurate and understandable; it provides a meaningful reference.
Artist (Drawing).failure: Key details are unclear or inaccurate; the diagram may mislead.
Artist (Drawing).phenomenal_success: The diagram is precise and beautifully clear; it provides a bonus to anyone using it as a reference.
Artist (Drawing).catastrophic_failure: The diagram contains a serious error that leads those relying on it astray.

Artist (Illumination).description: The character applies gold leaf, vivid pigments, and intricate patterns to a manuscript page.
Artist (Illumination).success: The page is beautifully illuminated; it enhances the manuscript's value and prestige.
Artist (Illumination).failure: The illumination is uneven or dull; the effect is mediocre.
Artist (Illumination).phenomenal_success: The page is a masterwork; it draws admiration and significantly increases the manuscript's worth.
Artist (Illumination).catastrophic_failure: The gold leaf is misapplied or the pigments bleed; the page is ruined.

Artist (Interior Decorating).description: The character arranges tapestries, furniture, and lighting to make a hall impressive and fitting for its purpose.
Artist (Interior Decorating).success: The hall is attractive and well-arranged; guests are impressed and the host's status is reinforced.
Artist (Interior Decorating).failure: The arrangement is dull or awkward; no particular impression is made.
Artist (Interior Decorating).phenomenal_success: The decoration is so tasteful and striking it becomes a talking point; host gains a reaction bonus.
Artist (Interior Decorating).catastrophic_failure: The arrangement is considered garish or improper; the host is embarrassed and may blame the character.

Artist (Painting).description: The character paints a formal portrait of a noble or wealthy merchant for display.
Artist (Painting).success: The portrait is a recognisable and flattering likeness; the patron is satisfied.
Artist (Painting).failure: The portrait fails to flatter or capture the subject; the patron is displeased.
Artist (Painting).phenomenal_success: The portrait is considered a masterwork; it greatly flatters the patron and may attract further commissions.
Artist (Painting).catastrophic_failure: The portrait is so unflattering or inaccurate the patron refuses to pay and the character's reputation suffers.

Artist (Painting).description: The character creates a devotional image — saint, icon, or altar panel — according to traditional conventions.
Artist (Painting).success: The icon follows the proper conventions and is considered devotionally appropriate.
Artist (Painting).failure: The image deviates from convention in ways the clergy notice; it is rejected.
Artist (Painting).phenomenal_success: The icon is deemed of unusual spiritual power and beauty; it is treasured.
Artist (Painting).catastrophic_failure: A serious error in iconography is considered blasphemous; the character faces censure.

Artist (Pottery).description: The character shapes clay on a wheel to produce a functional jar, bowl, or cup.
Artist (Pottery).success: The vessel is well-shaped, even, and suitable for use.
Artist (Pottery).failure: The piece collapses or cracks in the kiln; it must be redone.
Artist (Pottery).phenomenal_success: The vessel is beautifully proportioned; it is of fine quality and commands a higher price.
Artist (Pottery).catastrophic_failure: The clay flies off the wheel or the fired piece shatters; material is wasted.

Artist (Scene Design).description: The character plans the backdrop, props, and staging for a performance or public ceremony.
Artist (Scene Design).success: The staging is appropriate and effective; it enhances the performance.
Artist (Scene Design).failure: The staging is awkward or insufficient; actors or participants are hindered.
Artist (Scene Design).phenomenal_success: The staging is memorable and striking; it receives praise and enhances the event's impact.
Artist (Scene Design).catastrophic_failure: A prop or structure fails during the performance, causing disruption or injury.

Artist (Sculpting).description: The character carves a figure from stone or wood for decorative or religious purposes.
Artist (Sculpting).success: The statue is recognisable, well-proportioned, and suitable for its purpose.
Artist (Sculpting).failure: The statue is crude and does not meet the patron's expectations.
Artist (Sculpting).phenomenal_success: The statue is a fine work; it is considered excellent craftsmanship and commands a high price.
Artist (Sculpting).catastrophic_failure: A chisel slip ruins the stone or wood; the material is wasted and must be replaced.

Artist (Woodworking).description: The character carves intricate patterns or scenes into wood panels for furniture, church interiors, or chests.
Artist (Woodworking).success: The carvings are clean and attractive; the item's value and beauty are enhanced.
Artist (Woodworking).failure: The carvings are rough and unimpressive.
Artist (Woodworking).phenomenal_success: The carvings are of exceptional quality; the item is considered a work of fine craftsmanship.
Artist (Woodworking).catastrophic_failure: A miscut splits the panel; the material is ruined.

Astronomy.description: Using star charts and observation, the character calculates the date and appearance of an eclipse, conjunction, or solstice.
Astronomy.success: The prediction is accurate to within an acceptable margin; the event can be anticipated.
Astronomy.failure: The prediction is off; the character misstates the date or appearance.
Astronomy.phenomenal_success: The prediction is perfectly precise; the character can prepare elaborate displays or rituals to coincide with it.
Astronomy.catastrophic_failure: The prediction is badly wrong; an important ceremony or agricultural decision based on it fails.

Astronomy.description: Using the night sky, the character determines cardinal directions and rough geographic position.
Astronomy.success: The character correctly identifies direction and approximate location.
Astronomy.failure: The character is uncertain; direction is off by a significant margin.
Astronomy.phenomenal_success: Navigation is precise enough to estimate distance from a known point.
Astronomy.catastrophic_failure: The character is badly confused; direction given is wrong by 90 degrees or more.

Astronomy.description: The character compiles observations into a yearly table of celestial events for agricultural or religious use.
Astronomy.success: The calendar is accurate and useful for planning festivals and plantings.
Astronomy.failure: The calendar has errors that cause minor misalignments with actual events.
Astronomy.phenomenal_success: The calendar is so precise it becomes a valued reference and enhances the character's reputation.
Astronomy.catastrophic_failure: A fundamental calculation error propagates through the whole calendar, making it unreliable.

Boating (Sailboat).description: The character adjusts sails and helm to make progress against an unfavourable wind by zig-zagging.
Boating (Sailboat).success: The boat makes reasonable upwind progress without losing way.
Boating (Sailboat).failure: The tack fails; the boat loses headway and is blown back.
Boating (Sailboat).phenomenal_success: The tack is so precise the boat makes excellent progress, essentially sailing as if the wind were favourable.
Boating (Sailboat).catastrophic_failure: The boat is caught in irons or the boom swings violently, injuring crew or damaging rigging.

Boating (Sailboat).description: The character steers a boat through fast water, rocks, and currents without grounding or capsizing.
Boating (Sailboat).success: The boat passes safely through the hazard.
Boating (Sailboat).failure: The boat grounds or is buffeted hard; it may take minor damage.
Boating (Sailboat).phenomenal_success: The boat shoots through with impressive speed and control, arriving well ahead of schedule.
Boating (Sailboat).catastrophic_failure: The boat strikes rocks or capsizes, spilling crew into the water and potentially sinking.

Boating (Sailboat).description: The character guides a vessel to a harbour or safe anchorage in dangerous weather.
Boating (Sailboat).success: The boat reaches safety with no serious damage.
Boating (Sailboat).failure: The boat cannot make port; it must ride out the storm at sea.
Boating (Sailboat).phenomenal_success: The boat is brought in swiftly and safely, crew and cargo unharmed.
Boating (Sailboat).catastrophic_failure: The boat strikes the harbour entrance or a shoal; serious damage and possible sinking.

Boating (Unpowered).description: The character rows a small boat or raft across a river, managing current and obstacles.
Boating (Unpowered).success: The crossing is completed efficiently; the landing is close to the intended point.
Boating (Unpowered).failure: The current or poor technique pushes the boat off course; the landing is some distance downstream.
Boating (Unpowered).phenomenal_success: The crossing is fast and precise; no time is lost and the exact landing point is hit.
Boating (Unpowered).catastrophic_failure: The boat is caught in a strong current or overturns, throwing occupants into the water.

Boating (Unpowered).action: Pole a Raft Through Shallow Water
Boating (Unpowered).description: The character uses a pole to push a flat-bottomed craft through a shallow river or marsh.
Boating (Unpowered).success: The craft moves steadily without grounding.
Boating (Unpowered).failure: The pole becomes stuck or the craft grounds; progress halts until resolved.
Boating (Unpowered).phenomenal_success: The character finds the best channel and makes excellent time.
Boating (Unpowered).catastrophic_failure: The pole snaps or the character loses footing and falls into the water.

Body Language.description: The character observes a person's posture, facial expressions, and gestures to gauge their emotional state.
Body Language.success: The character accurately reads whether the person is calm, nervous, angry, or lying.
Body Language.failure: The character misreads the person's state or gets no useful information.
Body Language.phenomenal_success: The character reads extremely subtle cues, gaining nuanced understanding of the person's feelings and intentions.
Body Language.catastrophic_failure: The character completely misreads the person, perhaps confusing fear for aggression or deception for sincerity.

Body Language.description: By watching how a person moves and holds their body, the character infers whether they are concealing a weapon.
Body Language.success: The character correctly identifies that the person is armed and roughly where the weapon is.
Body Language.phenomenal_success: The character pinpoints the exact weapon and notes the person's readiness to use it.
Body Language.catastrophic_failure: The character incorrectly concludes the person is armed (or unarmed), potentially causing an incident.

Brawling.description: The character delivers a basic unarmed punch to an opponent.
Brawling.success: The punch connects, dealing crushing damage based on ST.
Brawling.failure: The punch misses or is blocked.
Brawling.phenomenal_success: The punch lands with perfect force; damage is maximised and the target may be stunned.
Brawling.catastrophic_failure: The character overextends, falls, or hits an unexpected surface, potentially injuring themselves.

Brawling.description: The character grabs an opponent and attempts to restrain them against a surface or on the ground.
Brawling.success: The opponent is grappled; they must break free to act freely.
Brawling.failure: The grapple fails; the opponent slips free.
Brawling.phenomenal_success: The opponent is pinned completely and cannot act until the character releases them.
Brawling.catastrophic_failure: The opponent reverses the grapple; the character is now the one who is pinned.

Brawling.description: The character delivers a forceful headbutt, usually in a close-quarters grapple.
Brawling.success: The headbutt connects and deals modest crushing damage.
Brawling.failure: The blow misses or glances off.
Brawling.phenomenal_success: The headbutt stuns the opponent for a second.
Brawling.catastrophic_failure: The character misjudges and staggers themselves, taking minor damage.

Breath Control.description: The character controls their breathing to stay submerged longer than normal.
Breath Control.success: The character doubles their normal breath-holding time, enabling extended underwater activity.
Breath Control.failure: The technique fails; breath-holding time is normal.
Breath Control.phenomenal_success: The character holds their breath for three times the normal duration.
Breath Control.catastrophic_failure: The character hyperventilates or panics, surfacing immediately or blacking out underwater.

Breath Control.description: Through controlled breathing and mental focus, the character suppresses visible signs of pain.
Breath Control.success: The character appears unaffected by pain; they can act normally for the current scene.
Breath Control.phenomenal_success: The character is so composed under pain that observers may question whether they were actually struck.
Breath Control.catastrophic_failure: The mental effort backfires; the character is actually more impaired by the pain.

Camouflage.description: The character disguises shelters, fires, and signs of occupation using natural materials.
Camouflage.success: The position is difficult to spot from normal observation distance; -4 to searches from afar.
Camouflage.failure: The camouflage is inadequate; trained observers may notice the camp.
Camouflage.phenomenal_success: The position is nearly invisible even to experienced trackers at close range.
Camouflage.catastrophic_failure: The camouflage effort leaves obvious disturbance; it actually draws attention to the position.

Camouflage.description: The character covers a person with foliage, mud, and appropriate colours to blend into the terrain.
Camouflage.success: The person gains a significant bonus to Stealth checks in that terrain.
Camouflage.failure: The camouflage is too inconsistent to be effective.
Camouflage.phenomenal_success: The person is so well concealed that even careful observers pass within arm's reach without seeing them.
Camouflage.catastrophic_failure: The camouflage uses plants or materials that are distinctive or cause irritation, drawing notice or causing discomfort.

Camouflage.action: Hide an Object in Plain Sight
Camouflage.description: The character disguises an item as something ordinary — a chest as furniture, a weapon as a walking staff.
Camouflage.success: Casual observers do not recognise the item for what it is.
Camouflage.failure: The disguise is insufficient; a close look reveals the object's true nature.
Camouflage.phenomenal_success: Even a deliberate search fails to identify the hidden object for what it is.
Camouflage.catastrophic_failure: The disguise is so obvious it draws more attention than leaving the item in the open.

Carousing.description: The character drinks, sings, and socialises to build goodwill with locals, soldiers, or merchants.
Carousing.success: The character makes a good impression; locals become friendly and may share useful information.
Carousing.failure: The evening passes pleasantly but without particular social gain.
Carousing.phenomenal_success: The character becomes the life of the evening; at least one NPC becomes a helpful contact.
Carousing.catastrophic_failure: The character drinks too much and causes an offence — insult, broken object, or fight — leaving enemies behind.

Carousing.description: The character socialises openly to pick up local rumours, news, and secrets from loose-lipped drinkers.
Carousing.success: The character learns one or two useful pieces of local information.
Carousing.failure: The character hears only mundane chatter.
Carousing.phenomenal_success: The character learns a significant secret or gains a crucial lead for their current task.
Carousing.catastrophic_failure: The character is fed deliberate misinformation, or makes enough noise to alert interested parties.

Carpentry.description: The character cuts, shapes, and fits wooden planks to create a functional and solid door.
Carpentry.success: The door is well-fitted and sturdy; it provides normal DR and HP.
Carpentry.failure: The door warps or fits poorly; it sticks or provides reduced DR.
Carpentry.phenomenal_success: The door is exceptionally well-made; its HP is above average and it fits perfectly.
Carpentry.catastrophic_failure: The door is so poorly constructed it falls off its hinges or splits on first use.

Carpentry.description: The character replaces damaged timbers and boards to restore a leaking or weakened roof.
Carpentry.success: The roof is sound again; the structure is weatherproof.
Carpentry.failure: The repair is only partial; some leaking persists.
Carpentry.phenomenal_success: The repaired section is stronger than the original; it will not require attention for many years.
Carpentry.catastrophic_failure: The character's work weakens a load-bearing element; the roof may collapse in heavy weather.

Carpentry.description: The character cuts and drives sharpened stakes to create a section of defensive palisade.
Carpentry.success: The palisade section is firm, even, and defensible.
Carpentry.failure: The stakes are uneven or poorly set; the section provides reduced defensive value.
Carpentry.phenomenal_success: The section is so sturdily built it is equivalent to a light wooden wall for defence purposes.
Carpentry.catastrophic_failure: The stakes are set too shallowly and topple under modest pressure.

Cartography.description: The character compiles survey data, terrain observations, and existing knowledge into a usable map of a region.
Cartography.success: The map is accurate enough to navigate by; major landmarks and routes are correctly placed.
Cartography.failure: The map has significant inaccuracies; distances or features are misleading.
Cartography.phenomenal_success: The map is of professional quality; it becomes a valuable resource and may be worth selling.
Cartography.catastrophic_failure: The map contains a serious error — reversed rivers, missing towns — that will lead travellers astray.

Cartography.description: The character reads and interprets an unfamiliar or old map to extract useful navigational information.
Cartography.success: The character understands the map's symbols and can extract relevant route or terrain data.
Cartography.failure: The character misreads conventions or cannot interpret the unfamiliar notation.
Cartography.phenomenal_success: The character extracts hidden or non-obvious information from the map, such as a secondary route.
Cartography.catastrophic_failure: The character completely misinterprets the map, leading to serious navigational errors.

Choreography.description: The character composes the steps and formations for a formal dance to be performed at a noble gathering.
Choreography.success: The dance is appropriate, graceful, and achievable for the performers.
Choreography.failure: The choreography is awkward or too complex; performers stumble during rehearsal.
Choreography.phenomenal_success: The dance is elegant and original; it is the talk of the gathering and reflects well on the patron.
Choreography.catastrophic_failure: A major choreographic error causes performers to collide or the routine to fall apart publicly.

Climbing.description: The character ascends a stone wall using handholds, a rope, or other aids.
Climbing.success: The character reaches the top safely.
Climbing.failure: The character cannot find a hold and makes no progress, or must retreat.
Climbing.phenomenal_success: The character scales the wall quickly and silently, arriving undetected.
Climbing.catastrophic_failure: The character loses their grip and falls from height, taking full falling damage.

Climbing.description: The character uses branches and trunk to ascend a large tree for concealment, reconnaissance, or retrieval.
Climbing.success: The character reaches the desired height safely.
Climbing.failure: The character struggles and makes slow progress.
Climbing.phenomenal_success: The character ascends swiftly and with great agility, reaching a high vantage point quickly.
Climbing.catastrophic_failure: A branch snaps; the character falls for significant damage.

Climbing.description: The character abseils down a steep face using a rope secured above.
Climbing.success: The character descends safely and in good time.
Climbing.failure: The character descends slowly and awkwardly; extra time is lost.
Climbing.phenomenal_success: The character reaches the bottom quickly and can immediately act.
Climbing.catastrophic_failure: The rope slips or the character loses control, plummeting to the bottom.

Concentration.description: The character performs a complex task — reading, calculating, crafting — while noise or chaos occurs around them.
Concentration.success: The character successfully ignores distractions and completes the task at full ability.
Concentration.failure: Distractions cause the character to make errors or take much longer.
Concentration.phenomenal_success: The character completes the task flawlessly in even less time than normal.
Concentration.catastrophic_failure: The character is so disrupted they must start the task over from the beginning.

Concentration.description: The character uses disciplined mental techniques to endure questioning without revealing sensitive information.
Concentration.success: The character gives nothing away during this session; the interrogator gains no new information.
Concentration.failure: The character's concentration slips; minor useful information may be extracted.
Concentration.phenomenal_success: The character remains perfectly composed, giving nothing away and possibly misleading the interrogator.
Concentration.catastrophic_failure: The character's mental defences collapse; they reveal more than intended.

Conducting.description: The character directs a group of singers or musicians through a rehearsal or live performance.
Conducting.success: The ensemble performs well; timing and tone are correct.
Conducting.failure: Parts of the ensemble fall out of time or pitch; the performance is mediocre.
Conducting.phenomenal_success: The performance is exceptional; audience members are moved and the ensemble gains reputation.
Conducting.catastrophic_failure: A serious conducting error confuses the ensemble; the performance collapses in disorder.

Cooking.action: Prepare a Meal from Limited Ingredients
Cooking.description: The character creates a nutritious and tasty meal using whatever is available in field or kitchen.
Cooking.success: The meal is palatable and nourishing; morale is maintained.
Cooking.failure: The meal is plain and unappetising; no morale benefit.
Cooking.phenomenal_success: The meal is surprisingly delicious; morale improves and those who eat it begin recovery with a bonus.
Cooking.catastrophic_failure: The character uses a bad ingredient or under-cooks meat; those who eat become ill.

Cooking.description: The character coordinates a multi-course feast for a noble gathering.
Cooking.success: The banquet runs smoothly; food is tasty and presentation is acceptable.
Cooking.failure: A course fails; food arrives cold or poorly seasoned.
Cooking.phenomenal_success: The banquet is lavish and memorable; guests are impressed and the host's status is enhanced.
Cooking.catastrophic_failure: A serious failure — spoiled food, fire in the kitchen — ruins the banquet and embarrasses the host.

Cooking.description: The character examines rations, meat, or stored food to determine if it is safe to eat.
Cooking.success: The character correctly identifies which food is safe and which is not.
Cooking.failure: The character is uncertain; they cannot determine safety reliably.
Cooking.phenomenal_success: The character identifies the exact nature of the spoilage or contamination.
Cooking.catastrophic_failure: The character declares spoiled food safe; those who eat it become ill.

Counterfeiting.description: The character casts or stamps a false coin using base metals to pass off as genuine currency.
Counterfeiting.success: The coin is convincing enough to pass casual inspection in trade.
Counterfeiting.failure: The coin is obviously wrong in weight or appearance; it will not pass.
Counterfeiting.phenomenal_success: The coin is nearly indistinguishable from genuine; even a money-changer is fooled.
Counterfeiting.catastrophic_failure: The coin is so obviously fake that anyone it is presented to immediately recognises the fraud.

Counterfeiting.description: The character creates a duplicate of a wax or metal seal for use on forged documents.
Counterfeiting.success: The copy is good enough to pass casual inspection.
Counterfeiting.failure: The copy has imperfections that a trained official would spot.
Counterfeiting.phenomenal_success: The copy is so exact even the seal's owner might not recognise it as a forgery.
Counterfeiting.catastrophic_failure: The seal copy is obviously wrong; documents bearing it are immediately flagged as forged.

Crewman (Seamanship).description: The character reduces sail area by folding and securing canvas as a storm approaches.
Crewman (Seamanship).success: The sails are properly reefed; the ship handles the storm safely.
Crewman (Seamanship).failure: The reefing is slow or incomplete; the ship is dangerously over-canvassed.
Crewman (Seamanship).phenomenal_success: The sails are reefed quickly and perfectly; the ship weathers the storm with no damage.
Crewman (Seamanship).catastrophic_failure: The character loses grip on the canvas in the wind; a sail rips away or a crew member is endangered.

Crewman (Seamanship).description: The character deploys the anchor correctly to hold a ship in position.
Crewman (Seamanship).success: The anchor sets well; the ship holds its position reliably.
Crewman (Seamanship).failure: The anchor fails to set; the ship drifts.
Crewman (Seamanship).phenomenal_success: The anchor sets on the first attempt in an ideal position; it holds even in a strong current.
Crewman (Seamanship).catastrophic_failure: The anchor chain snags or is lost; the ship drifts dangerously.

Cryptography.description: The character writes a message in cipher so that only those with the key can read it.
Cryptography.success: The message is correctly encoded; a casual reader cannot decipher it.
Cryptography.failure: The encoding contains errors that may confuse the intended recipient.
Cryptography.phenomenal_success: The encoding is flawless and uses a variant that provides extra difficulty even for skilled cryptanalysts.
Cryptography.catastrophic_failure: The character uses the wrong key or makes a systematic error; the message is undecodable by anyone.

Cryptography.description: The character analyses a ciphered document to determine its contents without possessing the key.
Cryptography.success: The character cracks the cipher and recovers the message's meaning.
Cryptography.failure: The cipher resists analysis; the message remains opaque.
Cryptography.phenomenal_success: The character decodes the message and identifies the cipher type, allowing future messages to be read faster.
Cryptography.catastrophic_failure: The character produces a plausible but completely wrong decoding of the message.

Dancing.description: The character dances a formal social dance at a noble gathering, following the expected forms.
Dancing.success: The character dances adequately; no embarrassment is caused.
Dancing.failure: The character stumbles or loses the rhythm, causing mild awkwardness.
Dancing.phenomenal_success: The character dances with grace and skill; observers are impressed and social standing rises.
Dancing.catastrophic_failure: The character badly stumbles, collides with another dancer, or commits a social error, causing embarrassment.

Dancing.action: Lead a Partner in an Unfamiliar Dance
Dancing.description: The character guides an inexperienced or uncertain partner through the steps of a dance.
Dancing.success: Both dancers complete the dance without incident; the partner is grateful.
Dancing.failure: The character's guidance is insufficient; the pair fumbles through the dance awkwardly.
Dancing.phenomenal_success: The character's expert leading makes even an inexperienced partner look skilled.
Dancing.catastrophic_failure: The character leads the partner into a stumble or collision, drawing laughter or scorn.

Detect Lies.description: The character closely watches a speaker for inconsistencies in tone, eye movement, or phrasing that indicate deception.
Detect Lies.success: The character correctly determines whether the person is lying.
Detect Lies.failure: The character gains no reliable reading.
Detect Lies.phenomenal_success: Not only does the character detect the lie, they also intuit approximately what the truth is.
Detect Lies.catastrophic_failure: The character mistakes truth for a lie or a lie for truth, drawing a completely false conclusion.

Detect Lies.description: The character listens to a witness's account and evaluates whether they are being truthful and complete.
Detect Lies.success: The character correctly judges the witness as truthful, evasive, or lying.
Detect Lies.failure: The character cannot reliably assess the witness.
Detect Lies.phenomenal_success: The character identifies exactly which parts of the account are false and which are being withheld.
Detect Lies.catastrophic_failure: The character dismisses a truthful witness or places full trust in a liar.

Diagnostics.description: The character examines symptoms to determine the nature of a disease or condition.
Diagnostics.success: The character correctly identifies the illness and its likely progression.
Diagnostics.failure: The character cannot determine what ails the patient.
Diagnostics.phenomenal_success: The character identifies the illness precisely, including its cause and an optimal treatment.
Diagnostics.catastrophic_failure: The character misdiagnoses the illness; treatment based on this diagnosis may make things worse.

Diagnostics.description: The character examines a corpse to determine how and approximately when the person died.
Diagnostics.success: The character correctly identifies cause of death and a rough time of death.
Diagnostics.phenomenal_success: The character finds subtle evidence of foul play, poison, or unusual cause that others would have missed.
Diagnostics.catastrophic_failure: The character reaches a completely false conclusion about the cause of death.

Diplomacy.description: The character represents one party in multi-session negotiations to reach a formal written agreement.
Diplomacy.success: A reasonable compromise is reached; both sides accept the terms.
Diplomacy.failure: Talks break down; no agreement is reached.
Diplomacy.phenomenal_success: The character negotiates terms surprisingly favourable to their side without alienating the other party.
Diplomacy.catastrophic_failure: The character causes grave offence; talks collapse and relations between the parties worsen.

Diplomacy.description: The character intervenes verbally when a dispute is on the verge of turning violent.
Diplomacy.success: Both parties stand down; the immediate crisis is averted.
Diplomacy.failure: The intervention fails; the confrontation escalates.
Diplomacy.phenomenal_success: The character's words turn hostility into grudging respect; the parties actually begin to listen to each other.
Diplomacy.catastrophic_failure: The character's words are taken as taking sides, inflaming both parties against each other and the character.

Diplomacy.action: Request an Audience with a Noble
Diplomacy.description: The character navigates protocol and courtly etiquette to formally request a meeting with a lord or official.
Diplomacy.success: The audience is granted; the character gains access.
Diplomacy.failure: The request is declined or ignored.
Diplomacy.phenomenal_success: Not only is the audience granted but it is scheduled promptly, and the noble enters the meeting with goodwill.
Diplomacy.catastrophic_failure: The character commits a serious protocol error; the noble is offended and the request is permanently refused.

Directing.description: The character coordinates actors, musicians, and stagecraft for a public theatrical production.
Directing.success: The performance proceeds without serious incident; audience is entertained.
Directing.failure: Coordination problems mar the performance; actors miss cues or sets malfunction.
Directing.phenomenal_success: The production is acclaimed; it builds the company's reputation and draws a large audience.
Directing.catastrophic_failure: The production is a disaster; major failures on stage humiliate performers and director alike.

Disguise.action: Disguise as a Different Social Class
Disguise.description: The character uses clothing, posture, and mannerism to convincingly pass as a peasant, merchant, or noble.
Disguise.success: Casual observers accept the character at face value in the assumed role.
Disguise.failure: Something is off; the character attracts a second look.
Disguise.phenomenal_success: Even close scrutiny from social peers of the assumed class fails to expose the disguise.
Disguise.catastrophic_failure: The disguise is obvious; the character is immediately questioned and their cover story may collapse.

Disguise.description: The character quickly changes clothing and alters hair or beard to escape identification by pursuers.
Disguise.success: Descriptions given to pursuers no longer match the character's current appearance; they pass searches.
Disguise.failure: The disguise is too superficial; the character is still recognisable.
Disguise.phenomenal_success: The character is so effectively transformed that even people who know them do not immediately recognise them.
Disguise.catastrophic_failure: The hurried disguise makes the character more memorable, not less, drawing extra attention.

Dreaming.description: The character deliberately enters a controlled dream state to work through a difficult problem or recall lost information.
Dreaming.success: The character gains useful insight or recalls a forgotten detail that aids their current situation.
Dreaming.phenomenal_success: The character achieves profound clarity; a crucial answer or hidden memory emerges with striking precision.
Dreaming.catastrophic_failure: The character has a deeply disturbing nightmare and wakes exhausted, suffering penalties to all actions until they rest.

Economics.description: The character analyses prices, supply, and demand in a local market to determine whether goods are over- or under-priced.
Economics.success: The character correctly identifies advantageous trading opportunities.
Economics.failure: The assessment is off; no particularly useful insight is gained.
Economics.phenomenal_success: The character identifies a market anomaly that could be exploited for significant profit.
Economics.catastrophic_failure: The character misreads the market badly, leading to a costly purchase or poor sale decision.

Economics.action: Estimate the Value of an Estate
Economics.description: The character inspects lands, buildings, and income records to estimate an estate's worth.
Economics.success: The valuation is accurate within 10%; it can be used in negotiations.
Economics.phenomenal_success: The valuation is precise and also reveals hidden assets or liabilities.
Economics.catastrophic_failure: The valuation is completely wrong; decisions made on it lead to financial loss.

Engineering (Artillery).description: The character calculates trajectory and adjusts the throwing arm angle for a desired target.
Engineering (Artillery).success: The shot lands in the correct general area; walls or defenders are threatened.
Engineering (Artillery).failure: The shot falls short or wide, wasting a projectile.
Engineering (Artillery).phenomenal_success: The shot is precisely calibrated and hits its target directly.
Engineering (Artillery).catastrophic_failure: A major miscalculation causes the projectile to land among friendly troops or demolishes the machine itself.

Engineering (Artillery).description: The character produces plans for a catapult, ballista, or trebuchet suitable for a campaign.
Engineering (Artillery).success: The design is functional; the engine can be built and will operate as specified.
Engineering (Artillery).failure: The design has flaws that reduce the engine's effectiveness.
Engineering (Artillery).phenomenal_success: The design incorporates clever improvements that increase range or damage beyond normal.
Engineering (Artillery).catastrophic_failure: The design has a critical structural flaw; the engine will fail catastrophically when used.

Engineering (Clockwork).description: The character diagnoses and repairs a failing gear train, escapement, or weight mechanism in a large clock.
Engineering (Clockwork).success: The clock keeps accurate time again.
Engineering (Clockwork).failure: The clock continues to run erratically.
Engineering (Clockwork).phenomenal_success: The repair improves the clock's accuracy beyond its original specification.
Engineering (Clockwork).catastrophic_failure: The character's intervention breaks a delicate component; the clock cannot function until a part is replaced.

Engineering (Clockwork).description: The character constructs a working automaton, music box, or mechanical toy from a design.
Engineering (Clockwork).failure: The device has minor functional defects.
Engineering (Clockwork).phenomenal_success: The device works flawlessly and shows exceptional craftsmanship.
Engineering (Clockwork).catastrophic_failure: A fundamental assembly error means the device will not function and may be damaged beyond repair.

Engineering (Combat).description: The character organises the digging of ditches, raising of earthworks, and placement of abatis for a defensive position.
Engineering (Combat).success: The fortifications are solid and provide meaningful cover and obstacle benefit.
Engineering (Combat).failure: The fortifications are hasty and inadequate; they provide minimal benefit.
Engineering (Combat).phenomenal_success: The position is strongly fortified; attackers suffer significant penalties to assault.
Engineering (Combat).catastrophic_failure: The fortifications are placed badly or structurally unsound; they hinder the defenders instead.

Engineering (Combat).description: The character directs miners or engineers to undermine and collapse a section of defensive wall.
Engineering (Combat).success: The wall section collapses as planned, creating a breach.
Engineering (Combat).failure: The mining fails; the wall holds.
Engineering (Combat).phenomenal_success: The collapse is larger than expected; a significant breach is opened.
Engineering (Combat).catastrophic_failure: The mine collapses prematurely or in the wrong direction, killing workers or causing no breach.

Engineering (Gunpowder).description: The character places and fuses a gunpowder charge beneath a gate, wall, or structure to destroy it.
Engineering (Gunpowder).success: The mine detonates at the correct time, destroying the target.
Engineering (Gunpowder).failure: The mine fails to detonate, or goes off too early with insufficient effect.
Engineering (Gunpowder).phenomenal_success: The detonation is precisely timed and positioned; the breach is wide and clean.
Engineering (Gunpowder).catastrophic_failure: The mine detonates prematurely, endangering or killing the engineer and nearby allies.

Engineering (Mining).description: The character designs a safe and efficient shaft or tunnel to access an ore deposit.
Engineering (Mining).success: The design is sound; the shaft can be excavated safely and efficiently.
Engineering (Mining).failure: The plan has flaws; the shaft requires revision, costing time.
Engineering (Mining).phenomenal_success: The plan is optimally efficient; the shaft reaches ore faster and at lower cost.
Engineering (Mining).catastrophic_failure: The shaft design leads into unstable ground; a collapse is likely during excavation.

Engineering (Mining).description: The character directs placement of timber supports to stabilise a dangerous mine tunnel.
Engineering (Mining).success: The tunnel is stabilised; work can proceed safely.
Engineering (Mining).failure: The shoring is insufficient; the tunnel remains dangerous.
Engineering (Mining).phenomenal_success: The shoring is so thorough the tunnel could bear heavy loads without risk.
Engineering (Mining).catastrophic_failure: Improper shoring triggers the very collapse it was meant to prevent.

Erotic Art.description: The character provides companionship and entertainment in a private setting to a person of means.
Erotic Art.success: The patron is satisfied and pays the agreed fee; no complications arise.
Erotic Art.failure: The patron is unimpressed; they pay little or nothing and are unlikely to return.
Erotic Art.phenomenal_success: The patron is deeply pleased; they become a regular and generous supporter, offering gifts or information.
Erotic Art.catastrophic_failure: The encounter goes badly wrong; the patron is offended or a complication arises with serious social consequences.

Escape.description: The character works their bound wrists or ankles to loosen and slip through rope restraints.
Escape.success: The character escapes the bonds and is free.
Escape.failure: The bonds hold; no progress made.
Escape.phenomenal_success: The character escapes so quickly that no one notices until they are long free.
Escape.catastrophic_failure: The attempt tightens the bonds; the character takes damage to their wrists and is more firmly restrained.

Escape.description: The character uses flexibility, improvised tools, or environmental features to get out of a secured space.
Escape.success: The character finds a means of egress and escapes.
Escape.failure: The room is too secure; no exit is found.
Escape.phenomenal_success: The character escapes and leaves the room looking undisturbed, concealing their escape.
Escape.catastrophic_failure: The escape attempt alerts guards or damages evidence the character needed.

Explosives (Demolition).description: The character places a measured powder charge against a fortified gate to blow it open.
Explosives (Demolition).success: The gate is breached; an assault party can enter.
Explosives (Demolition).failure: The charge is insufficient; the gate is damaged but holds.
Explosives (Demolition).phenomenal_success: The gate is completely destroyed; no obstacle remains.
Explosives (Demolition).catastrophic_failure: The charge detonates prematurely or wildly, injuring the character and companions.

Falconry.description: The falconer releases a trained hawk to chase and stoop on quarry such as rabbits or partridges.
Falconry.success: The hawk makes a successful kill and returns to the glove.
Falconry.failure: The hawk misses its quarry or is reluctant to return.
Falconry.phenomenal_success: The hawk makes a spectacular kill and returns immediately; it may even flush more prey.
Falconry.catastrophic_failure: The hawk refuses to return and is lost, or injures itself in a failed stoop.

Falconry.description: The falconer works daily to man (tame) and train a newly acquired hawk to the fist and lure.
Falconry.success: The hawk makes a measurable step toward being fully trained.
Falconry.failure: Today's session produces no progress; the hawk remains wild.
Falconry.phenomenal_success: The hawk makes extraordinary progress; training time is shortened.
Falconry.catastrophic_failure: The hawk bates violently and injures itself, or breaks a feather, setting training back significantly.

Farming.description: The farmer examines and tests soil to determine what crops will grow best and whether it needs amendment.
Farming.success: The farmer correctly assesses the soil; planting recommendations are sound.
Farming.failure: The assessment misses key factors; planting decisions are suboptimal.
Farming.phenomenal_success: The farmer identifies an ideal crop rotation that will significantly improve yields over several years.
Farming.catastrophic_failure: The farmer badly misjudges the soil; the recommended crop fails entirely.

Farming.description: The character organises and carries out the harvest of grain or vegetables before weather turns.
Farming.success: The harvest is completed on time; full yield is secured.
Farming.failure: Harvest is slow; some crop is lost to weather or over-ripening.
Farming.phenomenal_success: The harvest is completed ahead of time with exceptional yield; surplus is available.
Farming.catastrophic_failure: Poor organisation results in a large portion of the harvest being ruined.

Farming.description: The character maintains and operates earthwork channels to water crops during dry periods.
Farming.success: Water is correctly distributed; all fields receive adequate moisture.
Farming.failure: The distribution is uneven; some fields receive too much or too little.
Farming.phenomenal_success: Water is distributed so efficiently that less is wasted and yields are above average.
Farming.catastrophic_failure: A channel breaks or is directed wrongly; fields are flooded or left dry at critical growth time.

Fast-Draw (Ammunition).description: The character reaches for an arrow and nocks it to the string in a single fluid motion.
Fast-Draw (Ammunition).success: The arrow is nocked and ready; the character may fire immediately.
Fast-Draw (Ammunition).failure: The arrow fumbles; a normal drawing action is needed.
Fast-Draw (Ammunition).phenomenal_success: The nocking is so fast the character gains an extra action this turn.
Fast-Draw (Ammunition).catastrophic_failure: The arrow is dropped or knocked loose; the character must recover it before firing.

Fast-Draw (Melee Weapon).description: The character draws their weapon from scabbard or sheath in a single swift movement as combat begins.
Fast-Draw (Melee Weapon).success: The weapon is drawn as a free action; the character can attack this turn.
Fast-Draw (Melee Weapon).failure: The draw is not fast enough; it takes the normal action.
Fast-Draw (Melee Weapon).phenomenal_success: The character draws and strikes in one motion; the enemy is caught flat-footed.
Fast-Draw (Melee Weapon).catastrophic_failure: The weapon catches in the scabbard; the character is unarmed and briefly unable to draw.

Fast-Draw (Thrown Weapon).description: The character draws a throwing knife and hurls it in a single continuous motion.
Fast-Draw (Thrown Weapon).success: The throw is made as a free action; the character can make another action this turn.
Fast-Draw (Thrown Weapon).failure: The draw is too slow; normal ready and throw actions are required.
Fast-Draw (Thrown Weapon).phenomenal_success: The throw is made before the target can react; they are denied their dodge roll.
Fast-Draw (Thrown Weapon).catastrophic_failure: The knife fumbles during the draw, cutting the character's own hand.

Fast-Talk.description: The character uses rapid, confident speech to convince a guard or gatekeeper to let them pass.
Fast-Talk.success: The guard is confused or convinced; the character passes without inspection.
Fast-Talk.failure: The guard is unconvinced and bars passage.
Fast-Talk.phenomenal_success: The character is waved through without any question and the guard does not remember the encounter clearly.
Fast-Talk.catastrophic_failure: The rush of words makes the guard suspicious; they detain the character and call for backup.

Fast-Talk.description: The character deflects an immediate accusation of wrongdoing with rapid improvised excuses.
Fast-Talk.success: The accuser is thrown off; the matter is dropped for now.
Fast-Talk.failure: The excuse is thin; suspicion remains.
Fast-Talk.phenomenal_success: The character turns the accusation back on the accuser or introduces a plausible alternative culprit.
Fast-Talk.catastrophic_failure: The fast-talk is so obviously dishonest it hardens the accuser's conviction; the situation escalates.

Filch.action: Steal an Item from a Person
Filch.description: The character surreptitiously removes a small object from another person's pocket, pouch, or belt while in close proximity.
Filch.success: The item is taken without the victim noticing.
Filch.failure: The character cannot find a safe opportunity or the attempt is clumsy.
Filch.phenomenal_success: The item is taken so cleanly that even the character appears to have never moved.
Filch.catastrophic_failure: The victim feels the attempt and raises an alarm immediately.

Filch.description: The character conceals a small object by quickly secreting it on their person when others look away.
Filch.success: The item disappears; observers see nothing.
Filch.failure: The motion is noticed; observers question what was taken.
Filch.phenomenal_success: The sleight is so smooth that even someone staring directly at the character did not see it.
Filch.catastrophic_failure: The character fumbles the item; it clatters visibly and all present see it.

First Aid.description: The character applies a bandage and pressure to stop bleeding from a wound.
First Aid.success: Bleeding is stopped; the patient recovers 1 HP and is stable.
First Aid.failure: The bandage fails to stop bleeding; the patient continues to lose HP.
First Aid.phenomenal_success: The wound is expertly bound; the patient recovers 2 HP and heals faster.
First Aid.catastrophic_failure: The bandage is applied incorrectly and the wound is made worse; the patient loses an additional HP.

First Aid.description: The character immobilises a fractured limb using improvised splints and bindings.
First Aid.success: The limb is correctly immobilised; the patient can be moved without worsening the break.
First Aid.failure: The splint is insufficient; the break may worsen with movement.
First Aid.phenomenal_success: The splint is so well applied it aids healing; the patient recovers faster than expected.
First Aid.catastrophic_failure: The limb is set at the wrong angle; the break worsens and permanent impairment is possible.

First Aid.description: The character applies emergency care to a dying or deeply unconscious person to prevent them from slipping further.
First Aid.success: The patient is stabilised; they will not die without further intervention.
First Aid.failure: The care has no effect; the patient continues to deteriorate.
First Aid.phenomenal_success: The patient regains consciousness briefly and their condition improves enough to survive without immediate surgery.
First Aid.catastrophic_failure: The treatment is counterproductive; the patient's condition worsens immediately.

Fishing.description: The character casts a line with bait or lure into a river or lake to catch fish.
Fishing.success: The character lands a useful catch sufficient for a meal.
Fishing.failure: Nothing bites; the session yields nothing.
Fishing.phenomenal_success: The catch is exceptional; enough fish are landed for several meals.
Fishing.catastrophic_failure: The line snaps, the hook is lost, or the character is so frustrated they disturb the water and drive fish away.

Fishing.description: The character constructs and places a wicker or net trap in a suitable spot to passively catch fish.
Fishing.success: The trap is set correctly and yields a catch when checked the next morning.
Fishing.failure: The trap is incorrectly positioned or made; no fish are caught.
Fishing.phenomenal_success: The placement is ideal; the trap catches double the expected number.
Fishing.catastrophic_failure: The trap collapses or is swept away; materials are lost.

Forced Entry.description: The character applies brute force to a locked or barred door to break it open.
Forced Entry.success: The door is broken open; the character can enter.
Forced Entry.failure: The door holds; the character fails to breach it this attempt.
Forced Entry.phenomenal_success: The door flies open instantly; the character can enter without losing any momentum.
Forced Entry.catastrophic_failure: The character's kick rebounds or they injure themselves against a reinforced door.

Forced Entry.description: The character forces open a window shutter or narrow opening for entry.
Forced Entry.success: The shutter yields; a small person can squeeze through.
Forced Entry.failure: The shutter is too strong or the latch holds.
Forced Entry.phenomenal_success: The shutter tears completely free, leaving a wide and accessible opening.
Forced Entry.catastrophic_failure: The noise of the failed breach alerts those inside or nearby.

Forensics.description: The character examines the physical evidence at a scene of violence, theft, or suspicious death.
Forensics.success: The character recovers useful evidence that supports or contradicts a suspect's account.
Forensics.failure: The examination reveals little of value.
Forensics.phenomenal_success: The character reconstructs the sequence of events with impressive accuracy.
Forensics.catastrophic_failure: The character contaminates or misinterprets evidence, leading the investigation astray.

Forgery.description: The character creates a convincing document granting the bearer permission to travel or enter a place.
Forgery.success: The document passes inspection by guards or officials.
Forgery.failure: The document has flaws that a careful reader might notice.
